'''Joseph Keyes''' (born 17 September 1995) is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} and {{rlp|so}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] and [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[London Skolars]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. Keyes also played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|third tier]] and the [[RFL Championship]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent time on loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] at the [[York City Knights]] and [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship|second tier]].

==Background==
Keyes was born in [[London Borough of Enfield|Enfield]], [[London]], England and is the son of London Broncos player welfare manager John Keyes.<ref name="The Rugby World Cup leaves rugby league fans with mixed emotions">{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2015/oct/21/rugby-world-cup-league-fans-mixed-emotions|title=The Rugby World Cup leaves rugby league fans with mixed emotions|publisher=Guardian|date=2015-10-15|accessdate=2016-02-08|newspaper=The Guardian|last1=Willacy|first1=Gavin}}</ref><ref name="Joe Keyes: London Broncos deal 'a dream' for stand-off">{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/27978929|publisher=BBC|date=2014-06-27|accessdate=2016-06-09|title=Joe Keyes: London Broncos deal 'a dream' for stand-off|newspaper=BBC Sport}}</ref> He played his youth rugby league at Brentwood Eels RLFC, before joining London Broncos Academy.

==Playing career==
===London Broncos===
Keyes made his début for the London Broncos in the [[Super League]] against the [[Widnes Vikings]] in 2014.<ref name="Super League: London Broncos 24-40 Widnes Vikings">{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/28006450|publisher=BBC|date=2014-06-28|accessdate=2016-06-07|title=Super League: London Broncos 24-40 Widnes Vikings|newspaper=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 17 August 2014, the London Broncos were 22 points behind early in the second half against Leeds, but fought back to claim their first win in 11 months with Keyes scoring a [[hat-trick]] in a man of the match performance. That was the London clubs only win of the 2014 season.

The 2015 season in the Championship saw the London side field 11 homegrown players over the season including Dollapi, Magrin, Macani, Wicks, Thomas, Everett, Burnett, D Williams, M Davis, Walker and Keyes but the club finished in the bottom half of the Championship and it seemed only Walker and Davis were ever likely to play at a higher level.

In 2016, Andrew Henderson signed half-backs Israel Eliab, William Barthau and Scott Leatherbarrow at the start of the season, plus Api Pewhairangi in May and Keyes was perhaps fourth or five choice for just two positions, and he pushed to leave the club.

The Ireland international played 28 games for the London Broncos, with about seven appearances in the [[Super League]] with the rest in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] between 2014 and 2016 before asking for a release.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.londonbroncosrl.com/news/london-broncos-release-joe-keyes|title=London Broncos release Joe Keyes|publisher=}}</ref>

===London Skolars (DR)===
Keyes spent time on dual registration in 2016 at the [[London Skolars]].{{citation needed|date=March 2024}}

===Bradford Bulls===
'''2016''' - [[2016 Bradford Bulls season|2016 Season]]
Keyes featured in the Championship Shield in Game 3 against [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and then to Game 6 against [[Workington Town]]

Following the Bradford clubs liquidation at the end of the season, Keyes re-signed with the new Bradford side.{{citation needed|date=March 2024}}

'''2017''' - [[2017 Bradford Bulls season|2017 Season]]
Keyes featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and Keighley. 
[[File:Joe Keyes.jpg|thumb|right|Keyes playing for the Bradford Bulls in 2017]]
Keyes featured in round 3 the [[Swinton Lions]] and then in round 5 against the London Broncos. He also played in round 7 against Dewsbury to round 11 against Featherstone then in round 13 Sheffield to round 16 against Dewsbury. He played in round 18 Rochdale to round 23 against the Swinton Lions. Keyes featured in the Championship Shield Game 1 [[Toulouse Olympique]] to Game 2 Oldham then in Game 5 [[Sheffield Eagles]] to Game 7 Rochdale. Keyes also played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup]] in round 4 against Featherstone. He signed a two-year extension with the Bradford side at the end of the season.{{citation needed|date=March 2024}}

'''2018''' - [[2018 Bradford Bulls season|2018 Season]]

After recovering from a knee injury sustained at the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]], Keyes featured in the pre season friendly against Dewsbury and the Keighley Cougars. Keyes featured in round 1 against York to round 8 against Doncaster then in round 12 [[North Wales Crusaders]] to round 18 against York. He played in round 21 against the [[North Wales Crusaders]] to round 25 [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]] then in the Semi Final against the same opponent to Final [[Workington Town]]. Keyes played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup]] in round 3 West Wales Raiders then in Round 5 against Warrington.{{citation needed|date=March 2024}}

At the end of the season Keyes signed a two-year extension with the Bradford club.

'''2019''' - [[2019 Bradford Bulls season|2019 Season]]

Keyes featured in the pre-season friendlies against Halifax R.L.F.C. and Dewsbury. Keyes played in round 18 Halifax R.L.F.C. to round 27 against Rochdale.{{citation needed|date=March 2024}}

===York City Knights (loan)===
On 14 April 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a short-term loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=York Press|date=14 Apr 2021|accessdate=14 Apr 2021|title=York City Knights secure Ireland international Joe Keyes on loan
|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/19233289.york-city-knights-secure-ireland-international-joe-keyes-loan/}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 26 June 2021, it was reported that he had signed for Bradford in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=26 Jun 2021|accessdate=27 Jun 2021|title=Hull KR half-back Joe Keyes heads out to Championship on loan|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-joe-keyes-loan-5579180}}</ref>

'''2021''' - [[2021 Bradford Bulls season|2021 Season]]

Keyes played in Round 12 against Batley to Round 14 Featherstone Rovers.

===Halifax Panthers===
On 14 October 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Oct 2021|accessdate=16 Oct 2021|title=Halifax fend off rival clubs to secure Joe Keyes|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/halifax-fend-off-rival-clubs-to-secure-joe-keyes/}}</ref>

On 17 July 2022, Keyes scored four tries and kicked nine goals in Halifax's 58-10 victory over [[Workington Town]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/championship-rl-results-amazing-20-point-comeback-and-four-try-haul-for-former-hull-kr-man-56182/|title=Championship RL Results: Amazing 20-point comeback and four try haul for former Hull KR man|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=17 July 2022 }}</ref>
[[File:Joe Keyes Halifax.jpg|thumb|right|Keyes playing for the Halifax Panthers in 2023]]

===Bradford Bulls (re-join)===
On 4 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=4 Jul 2024|accessdate=26 Oct 2024|author=Nathan Atkinson|title=Bradford Bulls to bring in Joe Keyes from Halifax Panthers|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/24431797.bradford-bulls-bring-joe-keyes-halifax-panthers/}}</ref>

==Ireland==
Keyes made his representative début for Ireland in the [[2015 European Cup]].<ref name="European Championship: Scotland v Ireland preview">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/scotland-vs-ireland/preview/44352|title=European Championship: Scotland v Ireland preview|publisher=BBC|date=2015-10-23|accessdate=2016-01-29}}</ref> He went on to make three appearances in the tournament.

In 2016 he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref>

He featured against [[Spain national rugby league team|Spain]] and [[Russia national rugby league team|Russia]] in Ireland's route to the World Cup.
